title: rhamnus cathartica var. sylvatica
intro: Rhamnus cathartica var. sylvatica, also known as alder buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa, and is widely cultivated elsewhere for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in anthraquinones, a type of glycoside that has been used for centuries to stimulate bowel movements, relieve constipation, and treat other digestive issues. The medicinal properties of alder buckthorn have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Alder buckthorn is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in capsule or tincture form, allowing users to harness its medicinal properties in various ways.
